papers.title_en: MosaicQuant: Inlier-Outlier Disaggregation for Unified 4-Bit LLM Quantization
papers.authors: Yangjia Hu, Haodong Wang, Zicong Hong, Qianli Liu, Quanxin Shou, Jian Lin, Song Guo, Xiaowei Shen, Xiangjun Huang, Dian Wang, Jian Yang
papers.evidence: 4比特大语言模型量化，内点-离群点分离
papers.tldr: 4-bit量化虽能降低LLM内存和加速推理，但难以同时表示密集的常见值（inliers）和稀有大值（outliers），导致精度下降。现有混合精度方法破坏低比特执行统一性。本文提出MosaicQuant，将权重矩阵量化为密集4-bit基座，对inliers忠实捕捉，outliers量化后引入稀疏4-bit残差分量补偿误差。同时提出ZipperEngine，通过重叠流水线将稀疏块计算融合到密集4-bit GEMM内核，实现表示和执行统一。实验表明，在LLaMA3和Qwen3上保持近FP16精度，相比W16A16基线加速最高1.24倍。
papers.abstract_en: 4-bit quantization significantly reduces the memory footprint and accelerates the inference of large language models (LLMs). However, its limited bit-width representation struggles to faithfully capture both dense common values (\emph{inliers}) and rare large-magnitude values (\emph{outliers}), causing substantial accuracy degradation. Existing mixed-precision methods mitigate this by retaining outliers in high precision, but at the cost of breaking the uniformity of low-bit execution, introducing precision conversion and extra data movement that undermine practical speedup. We propose \textbf{MosaicQuant}, a unified 4-bit LLM quantization paradigm built on a novel principle of \emph{inlier--outlier disaggregation}. Rather than elevating outlier precision, MosaicQuant quantizes the full weight matrix into a dense 4-bit base component, where inliers are captured faithfully while outlier are inevitably quantized. A sparse 4-bit residual component is then introduced to compensate for these quantization errors, selectively targeting the most error-critical weight blocks where output distortion is shown to be concentrated. However, a unified representation alone is insufficient, as naïvely executing the sparse residual as a separate kernel still breaks the unified low-bit inference pipeline. To bridge this gap, we introduce \textbf{ZipperEngine}, which fuses sparse block computation into the dense 4-bit GEMM kernel via an overlapped pipeline, unifying not only the representation but also the execution into a single coherent low-bit inference pipeline. Extensive experiments on LLaMA3 and Qwen3 demonstrate that MosaicQuant preserves near-FP16 accuracy while achieving up to $1.24\times$ speedup over the W16A16 baseline.

papers.title_en: SoftMoE: Soft Differentiable Routing for Mixture-of-Experts in LLMs
papers.authors: Mikołaj Zasada, Łukasz Struski, Jacek Tabor, Marcin Kurdziel
papers.evidence: 软可微分MoE路由方法提升推理效率
papers.tldr: 大型语言模型中的稀疏MoE通过top-k离散路由仅激活部分专家以节省计算，但该操作不可微，导致专家分配固定且计算资源利用不均。针对此，SoftMoE引入基于截断soft top-k LapSum松弛的可微路由机制，并让模型自适应学习每层应激活的专家平均数量，同时施加全局预算约束。实验结果表明，在自回归语言建模和多个下游任务上，SoftMoE性能与稀疏MoE相当甚至更优，而激活的专家数大幅减少；学习到的分配高度非均匀，深层网络倾向于使用更多专家。该工作提供了可微的专家路由方案，实现了计算资源的自适应分配，有效提升了MoE推理效率。
papers.abstract_en: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures enable scaling LLM parameters under a fixed inference budget by activating only a small subset of experts via top-$k$ routing. While this preserves causality and suits autoregressive language models, the discrete top-$k$ operator is not differentiable, forcing a fixed number of active experts per input and resulting in inefficient use of computation. We propose SoftMoE, which replaces discrete routing with a truncated soft top-$k$ LapSum relaxation, allowing gradient-based optimization of expert routing. We further parameterize the mean number of active experts per layer and impose a global budget constraint, enabling the model to learn how to allocate expert capacity across layers. SoftMoE remains fully compatible with autoregressive modeling and achieves performance comparable to or better than sparse MoE on language modeling and downstream tasks, while activating significantly fewer experts. Notably, the learned allocation is highly non-uniform, with later layers activating more experts. The source code is publicly available$^\dagger$.
